title: More Nonsense Limerick 66
content:
There was an old person of Cannes,
    Who purchased three fowls and a fan;
    Those she placed on a stool, 
    And to make them feel cool
    She constantly fanned them at Cannes.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Edward Lear, titled "More Nonsense Limerick 66", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
